title: koyamacalia pilgeriana
intro: Koyamacalia pilgeriana, also known as Koyama's Aster, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to China and Taiwan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. Koyamacalia pilgeriana is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting immune function. It is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal purposes, although its herbal preparations are not widely studied or documented in modern medicine.
